description: Papers from 2020-2026 on contextual bandit algorithms applied to healthcare domains beyond physical activity.

papers.label: IntelligentPooling: Practical Thompson Sampling for mHealth
papers.authors: Sabina Tomkins, Peng Liao, Predrag Klasnja, Susan Murphy
papers.headline: Practical Thompson sampling contextual bandit for mHealth, deployed in a clinical trial.
papers.detail: Proposes IntelligentPooling for small sample sizes, non-stationarity, and delayed rewards in mHealth.
papers.notes: Core methodology from Murphy lab. Foundation for mHealth bandit deployments.

papers.label: A Contextual-Bandit-Based Approach for Informed Decision-Making in Clinical Trials
papers.authors: Yogatheesan Varatharajah, Brent Berry
papers.headline: CB with Thompson sampling outperforms random assignment in stroke clinical trial simulation.
papers.detail: Evaluated on International Stroke Trial data. 72.63% gain vs random, 64.34% gain vs context-free MAB.
papers.notes: Demonstrates clinical trial optimization using patient contexts.

papers.label: Brief Digital Interventions for Psychological Distress: An AI-Enhanced Response-Adaptive RCT
papers.authors: Jill Newby, Sunil Gupta, Leonard Hoon, et al.
papers.headline: Contextual MAB allocated 1,282 students to digital mental health interventions; physical activity best for severe distress.
papers.detail: AI-enhanced response-adaptive RCT. Physical activity and mindfulness effective for severe distress; physical activity and sleep hygiene for mild distress. Improved trial efficiency.
papers.notes: Landmark bandit clinical trial in mental health. Top medical journal.

papers.label: Protocol for the Vibe Up bandit-based adaptive trial for psychological distress
papers.authors: Kit Huckvale, Leonard Hoon, Eileen Stech, et al.
papers.headline: Protocol for contextual bandit AI-adaptive trial comparing digital interventions for student distress.
papers.detail: Multiarm contextual bandit with up to 12 sequential mini-trials comparing mindfulness, physical activity, sleep hygiene, and control.
papers.notes: Protocol for Vibe Up. Companion to the JAMA Network Open results.

papers.label: CAREForMe: Contextual MAB Recommendation Framework for Mental Health
papers.authors: Sheng Yu, Narjes Nourzad, Randye J. Semple, et al.
papers.headline: Contextual MAB framework for mental health with mobile sensing and online learning.
papers.detail: Integrates mobile sensing, online learning, and user clustering. Implemented across Discord and Telegram.
papers.notes: Framework paper; no clinical deployment.

papers.label: Using Adaptive Bandit Experiments to Increase Engagement in Mental Health
papers.authors: Harsh Kumar, Tong Li, Jiakai Shi, et al.
papers.headline: Thompson Sampling bandit deployed in text-message mental health intervention with 1,100 users.
papers.detail: Two-year system development. Recruited via large mental health non-profit. Compares bandit with uniform random.
papers.notes: Large-scale mental health deployment. Mohr and Williams labs.

papers.label: Offline Contextual MAB for mHealth: A Case Study on Emotion Regulation
papers.authors: Mawulolo K. Ameko, Miranda L. Beltzer, Lihua Cai, et al.
papers.headline: First offline CB recommender for emotion regulation using real mobile health data (n=114).
papers.detail: Doubly robust offline learning algorithms significantly outperformed baselines for emotion regulation strategy recommendation.
papers.notes: Early CB work for mental health. Transdiagnostic emotion regulation focus.

papers.label: Exploring RL for Fluid Transitions Between Clinical Mental Healthcare and Everyday Wellness
papers.authors: Tony Wang, Qian Yang
papers.headline: Contextual bandit for clinical-wellness mental health transitions (N=38); benefits appeared only post-intervention.
papers.detail: Contextual bandit selecting journaling prompts from clinical and wellness repertoires. Benefits of RL optimization emerged only after interventions ended. Raises questions about burnout and treatment intensity.
papers.notes: Important negative finding: bandit-optimized sequences showed delayed benefits.

papers.label: Personalized Glucose Management With AI: Pilot Study Using a Multiarmed Bandit Approach
papers.authors: Shinji Hotta, Mikko Kytö, Saila Koivusalo, Seppo Heinonen, Pekka Marttinen
papers.headline: Multi-armed bandit for postprandial glucose management shows 23% improvement in real-world pilot (n=6).
papers.detail: Two-stage reward prediction model predicts dietary and exercise effects on glucose. Simulation study plus 6-person pilot.
papers.notes: Early-stage diabetes bandit. Small but promising pilot.

papers.label: Equitable Intervention Planning with RMAB for Type 2 Diabetes Digital Health
papers.authors: Jackson A. Killian, Manish Jain, Yugang Jia, et al.
papers.headline: Restless MAB with equity constraints for T2D: 10% more patients reach targets, 85% less disparity.
papers.detail: RMAB with equitable bilevel optimization. 10% improvement in glucose targets, 10% reduction in dropout, 85% reduction in disparities across 6 demographic groups.
papers.notes: Important equity-focused bandit. Google-Verily-Harvard collaboration.

papers.label: Practical considerations for online learning algorithm in app-based mHealth (LOWSALT4LIFE 2)
papers.authors: Rachel T. Gonzalez, Madeline R. Abbott, Brahmajee Nallamothu, et al.
papers.headline: LOWSALT4LIFE 2 trial: contextual bandit for hypertension app engagement; deployment challenges documented.
papers.detail: RL algorithm sends notifications to reduce sodium intake. Documents challenges: reward definition, timescale, model specification, missing data, computational cost.
papers.notes: Important practical deployment paper. Hypertension domain. University of Michigan.

papers.label: REINFORCE trial: RL to personalize communication on medication adherence
papers.authors: John C. Lauffenburger, Elad Yom-Tov, Punam A. Keller, et al.
papers.headline: REINFORCE trial: RL-personalized communication significantly improved medication adherence at 6 months.
papers.detail: Multi-arm bandit RL trial testing personalized messages for medication adherence. Found significant improvements over 6-month follow-up.
papers.notes: Landmark bandit trial for medication adherence. Top digital health journal.

papers.label: RL for persuading to quit smoking and be physically active
papers.authors: N. Albers, M.A. Neerincx, W.P. Brinkman
papers.headline: RL algorithm modeling current and future states for smoking cessation and physical activity persuasion.
papers.detail: Models both current and future user states to personalize smoking cessation and physical activity persuasion strategies.
papers.notes: Smoking cessation bandit/RL. Combines smoking and physical activity.

papers.label: A Bayesian-bandit adaptive design for N-of-1 clinical trials
papers.authors: S. Shrestha, S. Jain
papers.headline: Thompson sampling bandit adaptive design for N-of-1 clinical trials.
papers.detail: Bayesian bandit using TS for individualized treatment comparisons within single patients.
papers.notes: N-of-1 trial design. Relevant for personalized medicine.

papers.label: Multi-disciplinary fairness considerations in ML for clinical trials
papers.authors: Irene Chien, Nina Deliu, Rebecca Turner, Adrian Weller, Sofia Villar, et al.
papers.headline: Multidisciplinary analysis of fairness in ML for clinical trials, including bandit algorithms.
papers.detail: Addresses fairness in bandit algorithm design, multiple testing, and ethical guidelines for clinical trials.
papers.notes: Directly addresses ethical concerns with bandits in clinical trials.

papers.label: Truthful mechanisms for linear bandit games with private contexts
papers.authors: Yiting Hu, Lingjie Duan
papers.headline: Shows UCB and TS fail under context misreporting in clinical trials; proposes truthful mechanism.
papers.detail: First study of private context misreporting in CB. Shows standard algorithms fail in clinical trial settings. Proposes LP-based mechanism with O(ln T) regret.
papers.notes: Important negative result: failure modes of standard bandits under strategic misreporting.

papers.label: A New Bandit Setting Balancing State Evolution and Corrupted Context
papers.authors: Alexander Galozy, Slawomir Nowaczyk, Mattias Ohlsson
papers.headline: Novel bandit combining contextual and state-transition info, evaluated on simulated medication adherence.
papers.detail: Combines contextual bandit and MAB via referee. Motivated by mHealth where user internal states are partially observable.
papers.notes: Handles partially observable internal states and unreliable context in mHealth.

papers.label: Optimal dose selection in phase I/II trials with contextual bandits
papers.authors: Jixian Wang, Ram Tiwari
papers.headline: CB with TS for oncology dose-finding; practical recommendations for industry adoption.
papers.detail: Extensive simulation comparing TS, Bayesian bootstrap, and MAB approaches for dose-finding. Practical recommendations provided.
papers.notes: BMS paper showing industry CB adoption for dose-finding.

papers.label: Learn to Intervene: Adaptive Learning for Restless Bandits in Preventive Healthcare
papers.authors: Arpita Biswas, Gaurav Aggarwal, Pradeep Varakantham, Milind Tambe
papers.headline: Restless bandit adaptive policy for medication adherence in diabetes and hypertension.
papers.detail: Adaptive learning policy for health intervention allocation in resource-constrained settings. Evaluated on Chiapas Mexico data.
papers.notes: Tambe lab. Restless bandits for global health.

papers.label: Optimizing HIV Patient Engagement with RL in Resource-Limited Settings
papers.authors: Álvaro Periáñez, K. Schmitz, L. Makhupula, et al.
papers.headline: Bandit algorithms for HIV patient engagement and treatment adherence in underserved populations.
papers.detail: Applies bandit algorithms to optimize HIV patient engagement in resource-limited Sub-Saharan African contexts.
papers.notes: Global health bandit application for HIV.

papers.label: Spoiled for choice? Personalized recommendation for healthcare: A multiarmed bandit approach
papers.authors: Tao Zhou, Yuqing Wang, Lu Yan, Yong Tan
papers.headline: Contextual bandit with diversity constraints for healthcare decision recommendations.
papers.detail: Multiarmed bandit for personalized healthcare recommendations with diversity constraints. Information systems perspective.
papers.notes: IS perspective on CB for healthcare recommendations.

papers.label: Risk-inclusive Contextual Bandits for Early Phase Clinical Trials
papers.authors: R. Kanrar, C. Li, Z. Ghodsi, M. Gamalo
papers.headline: Risk-inclusive CB with Thompson sampling for early phase clinical trials (Pfizer).
papers.detail: Multi-objective optimization with risk constraints. Leverages TS and online RL for early phase trials.
papers.notes: Industry (Pfizer) risk-aware bandits for clinical trials.

papers.label: Optimizing Digital Therapeutic Interventions under Endogenous Adherence
papers.authors: E. Pulick, S. Carpenter, M. Buman, Y. Mintz
papers.headline: UCB-BOLD: contextual bandit for digital therapeutics accounting for endogenous adherence.
papers.detail: Addresses the challenge that adherence is affected by the treatment itself (endogeneity).
papers.notes: Addresses endogeneity challenge in digital therapeutic bandits.

papers.label: Planning to fairly allocate: Probabilistic fairness in restless bandits
papers.authors: Christina Herlihy, A. Prins, A. Srinivasan, et al.
papers.headline: Probabilistic fairness framework for restless bandits in health intervention allocation.
papers.detail: Addresses ethical concerns about disparities from bandit-based health resource allocation. Provides fairness guarantees.
papers.notes: Fairness/ethics of bandit allocation in healthcare.

summary.key_findings: Contextual bandits are increasingly deployed in healthcare beyond physical activity, with major trials in mental health (N=1,282), medication adherence (REINFORCE), and clinical trial optimization. Key trends: Thompson sampling dominates; fairness/equity constraints emerging; practical deployment challenges documented; negative results highlight need for careful evaluation.
